```python
class Solution:
    def compareVersion(self, version1: str, version2: str) -> int:
        v1 = version1.split('.')
        v2 = version2.split('.')
        for i in range(max(len(v1), len(v2))):
            i1 = int(v1[i]) if i < len(v1) else 0
            i2 = int(v2[i]) if i < len(v2) else 0
            if i1 < i2:
                return -1
            elif i1 > i2:
                return 1
        return 0
```
solution: 
```python
class Solution:
    def compareVersion(self, version1: str, version2: str) -> int:
        v1 = version1.split('.')
        v2 = version2.split('.')
        for i in range(max(len(v1), len(v2))):
            i1 = int(v1[i]) if i < len(v1) else 0
            i2 = int(v2[i]) if i < len(v2) else 0
            if i1 < i2:
                return -1
            elif i1 > i2:
                return 1
        return 0
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(max(m, n))
space complexity: O(max(m, n))
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution splits the version strings into lists of revisions, iterates through them, and compares corresponding revisions as integers. It handles cases where one version string has fewer revisions by treating missing revisions as 0. This approach is efficient as it has a linear time complexity and uses space proportional to the length of the longer version string.
